channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: US messages via Witkoff and intermediaries are not negotiations: Araghchi
item.description: Iran's foreign minister says message exchanges continue with Washington, but insists there are no negotiations or trust.

item.title: War on Iran: Three key takeaways from Araghchi’s interview with Al Jazeera
item.description: Araghchi confirmed direct talks with Witkoff but denied that they constituted negotiations in an exclusive interview.

item.title: Israeli police inspect damage on the road after missile impact
item.description: Police inspect a strike site in Tel Aviv after a missile hit the city.

item.title: Iran attacks cause fire in Kuwait, Bahrain; kill man in UAE
item.description: Shrapnel from an Iranian drone interception kills a Bangladeshi national in the UAE's Fujairah city.

item.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 33 of US-Israel attacks?
item.description: Trump says US could leave Iran war in 2-3 weeks without a deal; Iran’s FM Araghchi says he has no faith in talks.

item.title: Al-Sharaa says Syria to stay out of war on Iran unless attacked
item.description: '14 years of war is enough for Syria': Ahmed al-Sharaa says Syria will remain outside the US-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Emergency workers search for victims of Israeli airstrikes on Beirut
item.description: Emergency workers searched through rubble in Beirut after Israeli airstrikes hit the Jnah neighbourhood.

item.title: Trump tells allies ‘get your own oil’, says Iran war could end in 2-3 weeks
item.description: Under pressure as domestic petrol prices soar, US president says no deal needed to end war on Iran.

item.title: Iran war live: ‘Missile from Iran’ hits oil tanker off Qatar’s coast
item.description: One of three missiles launched from Iran hit an oil tanker off Qatar's coast, no injuries reported.

item.title: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
item.description: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?
